| Tab | Column | Description | nan |
| User persona | User Type | Field staff, Data entry staff, Managers, Administrators, M&E team | nan |
| User persona | Description |  1. The user's background,-  2. Their work responsibilities and  3. How the system will help them, and what activities they should be able to do with it | nan |
| User persona | Number | Field Team- Min of 16, Manager- Min 5,, Administrator- Min 6, M&E- Min of 3 | nan |
| W3H | What | This is an activity that is performed, and a part of data is being collected.   Examples: Beneficiary Registration (the What) | nan |
| W3H | When | When is the activity described under "What" conducted. eg:  Sessions on the field 1. "When the field worker does a monthly household visit"- For every group usually 3 sessions every week for a group. So sessions run almost everday 2. "When a beneficiary calls mentioning there is an emergency"-- When the session are cancelled | nan |
| W3H | Who | This tells which person or role is responsible for doing the activity.  It should match with one of the user personas (like Field Worker, Coordinator etc.)  Think of “Who” as: Which person is doing this work? | nan |
| W3H | How | Avni has a few modes of data entry.  1. Field app (Android app) 2. Data Entry App (Browser based app) | nan |
| Form | Datatype | https://avni.readme.io/docs/concepts | nan |
| Form | Mandatory | If that particular question mandatorly needs to be answered without which you cannot save the form. If Mandatory is No user can save the form without filling the details | nan |
| Form | User entered / System generated | Indicates whether a question must be answered before the form can be saved.  If Mandatory = Yes, the user cannot save the form without filling in this field.  If Mandatory = No, the user can save the form even if the field is left blank. | nan |
| Form | Option condition/Validation | Include any field-level conditions, validations, or rules. Examples:  “Show this field only if Gender = Female”  “Mandatory if age < 5 years” | nan |

| Project Overview Details | Unnamed: 1 |
| ------------------------ | ---------- |
| Geographical location | Bengaluru, Kolar |
| Location heirarchy | Bengaluru (HO) |
| Where did you get to know about Avni | From Dalgo Bootcamp |
| Previous System of Data Management | Google Drive, Excel, Kobotool |
| Challenges with existing system | Management, Maintenance , visuals, retrieval and assessment |
| Reason for shifting to Avni | Efficiency, M&E, Streamlined Coordination Across Teams better resouce management |
| Any data to be migrated / imported from other system. If yes, provide details | Yes. From Google drives and Kobotool last 1 year |
| Any date you are aiming to roll out this in Avni | 1st week of Feb 2026 |
| Number of Users  | 25 |
| Program Details (one by one below) - Episodic Intervention with Beneficiary with a Start and End Date | nan |
| Name of the program | Work With Men |
| Objective of the program | Building public spaces free from sexual harassment |
| Who is Eligible for this program intervention | Formal and Informal sector (Ex. sanitation workers,  Law and Order, public transport staff, Auto drivers) |
| When would a person get enrolled into program | Start of the program |
| When would a person get exited from the program | 2 months to 1.5 years from the start of the program |
| Total number of expected beneficiaries in next one year | Approx 3000 in a year |
| Digitisation success indicators | % of intended users adapting to it with ease and minimum data errors, Reduction in time to complete key tasks,  |
| Key success indicators on this program | Ease in understanding the data at program and organisation level, User Satisfaction, Ability to align to DTDP |
| List the forms with description in following format - Name of the form - Key data elements captured - When does form get filled | Cohort Details Group Details Participant Details Session Details Assessment Details )M&E) Reports Dashboard |

| List the reports / Describe each report | Reports on Cohorts, Groups, Assessments, Participants at program level and at Organisation Level |
| Share format of reports, if available | nan |
| Name of the program | Work With Women |
| Objective of the program | Building public spaces free from sexual harassment |
| Who is Eligible for this program intervention | Formal and Informal sector (Ex. sanitation workers,  Domestic Helpers, Security Staff, Auto drivers) |
| When would a person get enrolled into program | Start of the program |

| Important Notes | 1. They will primarily use the DEA.  2. Baseline and endline assessments will be conducted only for a randomly selected subset of participants, not for all participants. For whom baseline is done only for them endline will be done  3. They plan to share the baseline and endline forms with participants as a link that they can fill out and submit. Alternatively, they may share the Kobo link with participants and later upload the collected data to Avni for the baseline and endline.  4. There is **no fixed geographic catchment** assigned to individual participants. Based on operational requirements, participants may travel across multiple locations as part of their work. |
| Product Feature Requirement | They are ~99% likely to use the DEA, so it’s important that all of these features work seamlessly on the DEA as well  Product Feature Requests (DEA-focused)  1. Add Members to a Group / Cohort - Screenshot attached where required  The system should allow facilitators to select multiple members at once.  2. Attendance Management with Pre-selection - Screenrecording attached where required  All members added to the Group should be marked as present by default in a Session  Facilitators should have the ability to deselect participants who did not attend.  3. Participant Detail Duplication & Auto-Fill  The system should support duplicating participant details when registering other participants   Since most participant details remain the same across sessions, the system should:  Auto-capture existing participant data except First Name and Last Name  Allow facilitators to edit only the fields that have changed (e.g., name, phone number, or other identifiers), without requiring re-entry of all information.  4. Form Link Sharing for Non-Avni Users The system should allow facilitators to generate and share form links (e.g., Baseline Assessment forms) with the participants who are not Avni users. These links should be shareable via WhatsApp or SMS. Once a participant submits the form, the data should be automatically captured and synced into Avni, mapped to the correct participant/cohort. |

| Cohort Registration | At the start of the Group | Field Assocates | Web brower (online) | Start of the Group | At regular intervals, a **specific cohort type** is selected, and a **series of sessions** is conducted with that group. Each cohort receives sessions covering **15 key topics** related to women’s safety, gender sensitivity, and practical actions that contribute to safer public spaces. | nan |
| Participant Details | At the start of the Group | Field Assocates | Web brower (online) | Start of the Group | If new particpants join, then it will added as when required | nan |

Behaviour Footprint Tracker , Stories of Change (Photos, Testimonials, Success stories. Videos) | nan |
| Cohort Endline | After all the sessions are completed, the field associate will fill the cohort closing form via web application from office | nan | nan | nan | nan | nan |

| Step No | Step Name | Proposed Solution | Phase | User | App | Status | Additional Notes | Reference Video |
| ------- | --------- | ----------------- | ----- | ---- | --- | ------ | ---------------- | --------------- |
| Step 1 | Cohort Registration | This can be filled via android app from the field or via web application from the office | Phase 1 | Field Associate | Web app (online) | Currently Possible |  DEA supports Cohort Registratytion as per requirement | nan |
| Step 2 | Participant Registration | via CSV Upload | Phase 1 | Field Associate | Web app (online) | Currently Possible | Avni currently does not have a duplicate participant data feature. Registering participants via CSV upload allows users to easily copy common details and reuse them for multiple records, reducing manual effort and chances of data entry errors. | nan |
| Step 3 | Add Cohort Members | via CSV Upload | Phase 1 | Field Associate | Web app (online) | Currently Possible | Adding around 70 members one by one is time-consuming and operationally inefficient. Avni currently does not support selecting and adding all members at once” in DEA or the mobile app. CSV upload allows multiple participants to be linked to the correct cohort in a single upload, significantly reducing effort and errors | nan |
| Step 4 | Session | This can be filled via android app from the field or via web application from the office | Phase 1 | Field Associate | Web app (online), Android app (offline) | Currently Possible |  DEA supports session delivery workflows as per requirement | nan |
| Step 5 | Cohort Endline | After all the sessions are completed, the field associate will fill the cohort closing form via web application from office | Phase 1 | Field Associate | Web app (online), Android app (offline) | Currently Possible | DEA currently supports Cohort Endline workflows as per the requirement | nan |
| Step 6 | Cohort Approval |  - The manager will see due approvals in the android app and will approve / reject the forms - If the cohort closing form is approved, then the numbers will reflect in the reports | Phase 1 | Manager | Android app (offline) | Currently Possible | Currently, DEA does not support the approval feature, so cohort approval needs to be done via the mobile application. The approval flow works better on the mobile app, as the mobile dashboard clearly shows pending approvals and approved cohorts. This gives managers better clarity on their tasks, including which cohorts need to be reviewed and approved. | nan |
| Step 7 | Baseline | Continue as is on Kobo Upload the datasheets on Avni | Phase 1 | Field Associate | Web app (online) | Currently Possible | nan | nan |
| nan | Baseline | By end of March / beginning April - Survey links sent to participants via mobile app - A bulk send button will be there. User will choose which all participant to send and based on that the link will be sent to each participant on their whatsapp | Phase 2 | Field Associate | Android app (offline) | New Feature | nan | nan |
| Step 8 | Endline | Continue as is on Kobo Upload the datasheets on Avni | Phase 1 | Field Associate | Android app (offline) | Currently Possible | nan | nan |
| nan | Endline | By end of March / beginning April - Survey links sent to participants via mobile app - A bulk send button will be there. An option will be there to filter those for whom the baseline has been filled. User will choose which all participant to send and based on that the link will be sent to each participant on their whatsapp | Phase 2 | Field Associate | Android app (offline) | New Feature | nan | nan |
